refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure operational availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target by minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery.
refined_fr.rationale: Availability KPI is decomposed to focus on minimizing downtime and enabling rapid recovery, which are key contributors to availability. This allows targeted design and implementation decisions.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure actual operational uptime and downtime over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: Enable rapid recovery from faults or failures

candidate_dps: Redundant system components and fault-tolerant design
Automated fault detection and recovery procedures

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically addresses both sub-FRs with minimal coupling to unrelated functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of reliable telemetry data for monitoring
Risk that predictive maintenance algorithms may not detect all failure modes
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating fault-tolerant hardware design in subsequent decomposition levels to complement monitoring.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Implement real-time monitoring and predictive maintenance algorithms to ensure system availability and safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time sensor and system status data for monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends alerts related to system faults impacting availability and safety.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates with other modules for coordinated fault recovery and status updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of monitoring logic.
Physical hardware focus; less suitable for software-intensive monitoring.
Some monitoring related to operator control but limited scope.
Highest containment of monitoring and safety-related availability functions.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Safety Services module best encapsulates the monitoring and predictive maintenance DP, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with Teleoperation for fault recovery coordination
Dependency on reliable network link may introduce diffusion
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify interface boundaries with Teleoperation to reduce diffusion and improve ownership clarity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FRs with clear rationale and manageable assumptions.

diffusion_risks: Network Link interface may cause diffusion between Safety Services and Teleoperation modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Safety Services module shows strong containment of the DP with minimal diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and safety alert logs is feasible and aligns with stakeholder needs.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of monitoring and maintenance functions to Safety Services module.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit interface contracts between Safety Services and Teleoperation to manage diffusion.
Plan for integration of fault-tolerant hardware DP in next decomposition level.

decision.justification: Current decomposition level sufficiently refines the availability FR with a clear DP and IM assignment that passes all gates. No immediate need to zigzag or revise at this level.
next_level_sub_frs: Enable rapid recovery from faults or failures

revise.why: No deficiencies identified requiring revision at this level.

assumptions: Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
open_risks: Potential diffusion via Network Link interface.
evidence_needed: Operational telemetry data demonstrating availability performance.
